daily_scan
Vision Zero projects updates: The Vision Zero initiative is working on transportation safety improvements, and public engagement is encouraged through various activities. Editor context: Vision Zero efforts are ongoing, impacting community safety with potential policy changes or new initiatives. Suggested treatment: watch. Newsworthiness: 12/20 (immediacy 3, impact 4, conflict 2, novelty 3). Why now: Vision Zero projects are ongoing, with activities and ways to get involved. Suggested next step: Check for recent project updates and public involvement opportunities. What would make this publishable: Detailed updates on specific projects, progress reports, or new initiatives.

Novelty 3/5. Vision Zero projects are ongoing, with activities and ways to get involved.

Confidence: med
Open draft
Risk: med
Background
Background
daily_scan
St. Vrain Valley Schools' Career Elevation & Technology Center Offers High-Demand Courses: The CETC provides high-wage, high-growth pathways that prepare students for future careers in technology and other fields. Editor context: This highlights a specific educational resource that could be of interest to local students and parents seeking career preparation opportunities. Suggested treatment: background. Newsworthiness: 10/20 (immediacy 2, impact 3, conflict 1, novelty 4). Why now: Details on the offerings and benefits of the Career Elevation & Technology Center. Suggested next step: Explore the curriculum offered at CETC to determine if it aligns with current labor market demands. What would make this publishable: More specific details about course content, enrollment numbers, and success rates.

Novelty 4/5. Details on the offerings and benefits of the Career Elevation & Technology Center.

Confidence: med
Draft anyway
Risk: med
Background
Background
daily_scan
St. Vrain Valley Schools Receives $53 Million in Competitive Grants: The St. Vrain Valley School District has received over $53 million in competitive grants since January 2018, indicating significant funding for new educational initiatives. Editor context: This financial information highlights substantial grant funding that could impact school resources and programs, making it relevant to local reporting. Suggested treatment: background. Newsworthiness: 10/20 (immediacy 2, impact 3, conflict 1, novelty 4). Why now: New financial information regarding competitive grant awards. Suggested next step: Research the specific grants received, their purposes, and any recent projects funded by these grants. What would make this publishable: Details on the specific grants, their sources, and how they are being used to improve educational programs.

Novelty 4/5. New financial information regarding competitive grant awards.

Confidence: med
Draft anyway
Risk: med
Watch
Watch
daily_scan
Board of Education Regular Meeting: The St. Vrain Valley School District Board of Education will hold a regular meeting on August 12 at 6:00 p.m., providing an opportunity for public input. Editor context: Public education matters to the community, and this meeting offers insight into district governance. Suggested treatment: watch. Newsworthiness: 10/20 (immediacy 3, impact 4, conflict 1, novelty 2). Why now: A regular school board meeting is scheduled, providing an opportunity for public engagement. Suggested next step: Confirm the exact location of the meeting before drafting a story or brief. What would make this publishable: The exact location and agenda of the upcoming Board of Education meeting.

Novelty 2/5. A regular school board meeting is scheduled, providing an opportunity for public engagement.

Confidence: med
Draft anyway
Risk: med
Draft exists
Brief
Ready to draft
daily_scan
Longmont Public Library hosting Teen Temporary Tattoo Studio: The Longmont Public Library is offering a Teen Temporary Tattoo Studio on Wednesday, July 1 from 5-6 pm, providing teens with an opportunity to get temporary tattoos. Editor context: This event provides information about activities for local youth and their families. Suggested treatment: brief. Newsworthiness: 10/20 (immediacy 3, impact 2, conflict 1, novelty 4). Why now: A new event has been added to the library's schedule. Suggested next step: Verify the location, time, and any specific requirements or materials needed for participants. What would make this publishable: Confirmation of the specific materials provided, any registration requirements, and target age group.

Novelty 4/5. A new event has been added to the library's schedule.

Confidence: med
Open draft
Risk: low
